### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf08679-task`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Read Metrics from Google Sheets

### Read Metrics from Google Sheets

googleSheets — `read` operation against the connected account.

### Compute Severity Signals

Custom JavaScript: Score each task 0-100 from simple heuristics

Next: Grade Severity Tier

### Grade Severity Tier

Routes each item to one of 4 lanes: info, warning, critical + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Just Record (Info) · ②→ Warn Channel (Discord) · ③→ Page Urgently (Slack) · ④→ Archive Unscored

### Just Record (Info)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Merge Tier Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Warn Channel (Discord)

discord — `action` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Merge Tier Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

### Page Urgently (Slack)

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Merge Tier Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Archive Unscored

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Merge Tier Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Merge Tier Outcomes

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Guard Non-Empty title

### Guard Non-Empty title

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Send Summary to Discord

### Send Summary to Discord

discord — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Discord

### Escalate Failure to Discord

discord — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

# Alert the team on new events with Discord at scale

**Pattern:** Event Notification · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Alert the team on new events with Discord at scale. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual task routing is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Read Metrics from Google Sheets → Compute Severity Signals → Grade Severity Tier → Just Record (Info) → Merge Tier Outcomes → Guard Non-Empty title → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to event notification without manual effort
- Anyone automating task routing in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Has retries and error handling built in

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Discord webhook URL; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your task routing process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
